facility_name: CyrusOne CHI1 — CME Group Exchange Data Center
customer_type_if_known: CME Group (derivatives exchange — CME Globex matching engine)
build_phase_if_known: Operational since 2009; cooling upgrades post-Nov 2025 outage
cooling_type: Chilled water — eight 540-ton chillers (4 compressors each), free cooling below 30F, CRAH units, evaporative condensing chillers with oil-free centrifugal compressors. Backup cooling added Dec 2025 after 10-hour outage.
opportunity_summary: CME Group's primary electronic trading platform (CME Globex) runs from this 428,000 sq ft Aurora campus. 92MW at 2N redundancy. Nov 27, 2025 chiller plant failure halted global derivatives trading for ~10 hours — temperatures exceeded 100F. CyrusOne added backup cooling capacity post-incident. High-density financial trading workloads make this a strong candidate for liquid cooling retrofit. CME sold to CyrusOne in 2016 for $130M under 15-year sale-leaseback.
confidence_reason: Baxtel facility page provides exact address, MW, cooling specs. DCD, Bloomberg, Network World confirm Nov 2025 outage and cooling upgrades. CME press releases confirm Globex location.
source_1_title: CyrusOne Chicago Aurora CME Data Center
source_1_evidence_snippet: 2905 Diehl Rd, Aurora, IL. Up to 184MW total (92MW @ 2N). 428,000 sq ft. Eight 540-ton chillers. CME Globex hosted on-site under 15-year lease.
source_2_title: Cooling issue at data center halts futures trading for CME Group
source_2_evidence_snippet: CyrusOne CHI1 facility experienced chiller plant failure, halting CME Group trading for nearly 10 hours.
notes: KEY FINANCIAL INFRASTRUCTURE. Cooling failure on Nov 27 2025 disrupted global markets. Backup cooling added post-incident. 16-acre campus. Dual 138kV transmission feeds. 54-hour generator run time. Strong retrofit opportunity — current air-cooled chiller system is vulnerable to cascading failures as demonstrated.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Operational since 2009. CME Globex primary site. Cooling upgraded post-Nov 2025 outage.

facility_name: Equinix NY5 — Financial Exchange Hub (ZutaCore Liquid Cooling)
customer_type_if_known: Major stock exchanges — matching engines for electronic trading
build_phase_if_known: Operational; ZutaCore two-phase liquid cooling in production
cooling_type: ZutaCore two-phase direct liquid cooling (dielectric fluid, evaporator chamber on CPU, vapor return to condenser at ~92F/33C). Also 146-ton water-cooled DX penthouse air conditioning (3+1 config). 4kW avg / 6kW peak per cabinet.
opportunity_summary: Equinix NY5 houses matching engines for world's leading stock exchanges. Running ZutaCore two-phase direct liquid cooling in production on Equinix Metal platform. Part of Equinix's 100+ data center liquid cooling rollout across 45+ metros. Financial trading workloads demand ultra-low-latency, high-density compute. ZutaCore HyperCool uses dielectric fluid that boils at 92F to remove CPU heat.

facility_name: Trane Technologies + LiquidStack — Immersion & Direct-to-Chip Platform
full_address: LiquidStack HQ, Carrollton, TX (+ Hong Kong operations)
customer_type_if_known: Data center operators globally (enterprise, hyperscale, AI/HPC)
build_phase_if_known: Acquisition announced Feb 2026, completed shortly after. LiquidStack in Trane Americas Commercial HVAC unit.
cooling_type: LiquidStack high-density liquid, direct-to-chip and immersion cooling. Central plant-to-chip integrated thermal management. Engineered for generative AI and hyperscale. Manufacturing + R&D in Texas and Hong Kong.
opportunity_summary: M&A MARKET SIGNAL: Trane Technologies acquired LiquidStack (Feb 2026) for end-to-end DC thermal management: chillers + heat rejection + controls + liquid distribution + on-chip cooling. LiquidStack provides high-density liquid, DTC, and immersion cooling for AI/hyperscale. Operations in Carrollton TX + Hong Kong. Now in Trane Americas Commercial HVAC unit. Also partner in Samsung+Chemours Opteon testing (48U tank).
confidence_reason: Trane Technologies investor relations press releases (announcement + completion), DCD, Facilities Dive, Business Wire all confirm. Primary sources from Trane.
source_1_evidence_snippet: Trane acquires LiquidStack for end-to-end DC thermal management. Chillers to chip-level cooling.
source_2_evidence_snippet: Trane acquires LiquidStack. High-density liquid, DTC, and immersion cooling for AI and hyperscale.
notes: M&A MARKET SIGNAL. Trane + LiquidStack = central-plant-to-chip thermal platform. LiquidStack also tested Samsung Opteon immersion fluid (48U tank). Operations: Carrollton TX + Hong Kong.
